# Chapter 3: Supply Pathways

## Synopsis

Chapter 3 examines how electricity generation, fuel supply, and low-carbon
hydrogen production must evolve under each scenario. It models the required
buildout rates for solar PV, wind, nuclear, and grid storage.

## Portfolio Lens

The energy-transition lens highlights that the supply transformation hinges on
three parallel buildout tracks: variable renewables, dispatchable low-carbon
capacity (nuclear + hydro + CCS), and grid infrastructure. Failure in any track
delays the whole transition.

## Key numbers and projections

- Solar PV capacity must reach 5,200 GW by 2030 under NZE (from 1,600 GW in 2023)
- Wind additions must average 320 GW per year 2024-2030 under NZE
- Low-carbon hydrogen production requires $200 billion per year in new investment

## Open questions

- Whether critical mineral supply chains can scale to meet solar and battery demand
- Grid integration costs for >80% variable renewable share at regional level
